description:
From /critical-review of specled_-csg, consensus finding F6 [Medium; spec+testing]. Sync.entries/2 halts the whole sync on the first invalid path or undecodable/future-schema entry ({:halt, {:error, ...}}) — one bad blob on the shared orphan branch (foreign tool write, future schema_version from a newer specled on a teammate's machine, manual damage) breaks evidence sync for EVERY peer until manually repaired. Skip-and-warn (carry unknown entries through untouched, or quarantine) instead of halting; spec the tolerance behavior in evidence_store.spec.md.
Files: lib/specled_ex/evidence/sync.ex:177-194; lib/specled_ex/evidence/entry.ex:42-54.
Full report: /Users/mz/vaults/rmbrs-vault/code_review/critical/specled_ex/2026-07-22/specled_-csg-evidence-ledger-split/20260722-062318-consolidated.md

comments.text:
ADMISSION SCOPE (orchestrator, run-2 W2)
Note: specled_-737 has landed on the epic branch — sync.ex is now rewired onto the shared SpecLedEx.Evidence.Git module; line numbers in the ticket body predate that refactor.
Allowed touches:
- lib/specled_ex/evidence/sync.ex
- lib/specled_ex/evidence/entry.ex
- .spec/specs/evidence_store.spec.md (spec the tolerance behavior, as the ticket requires)
- test/specled_ex/evidence/** (sync/entry tests)
Out of scope (intent): one anomalous blob (invalid path, undecodable entry, future schema_version, foreign write) must no longer halt sync for every peer — skip-and-warn or quarantine, carrying unknown entries through untouched. Preserve the ADR safety property: evidence never gates; misses fail safe. Do not touch tree_hash.ex/git.ex (owned by specled_-43y this wave) beyond what compilation requires — disclose any such touch in IMPLEMENTED.

IMPLEMENTED
Session: (not assigned in delegation prompt)
Log: (not provided)

Commit: fdaac42
Files touched: lib/specled_ex/evidence/sync.ex, lib/specled_ex/evidence/entry.ex, .spec/specs/evidence_store.spec.md, test/specled_ex/evidence/sync_test.exs, test/specled_ex/evidence/entry_test.exs
Tests added: SpecLedEx.Evidence.SyncTest "quarantines an invalid path, unparsable JSON, and a future schema version without halting"; SpecLedEx.Evidence.SyncTest "quarantined entries independently written at the same path converge deterministically"; SpecLedEx.Evidence.EntryTest "distinguishes malformed JSON and unsupported schema versions from other decode failures". Adapted (not deleted): SpecLedEx.Evidence.EntryTest "validates entry filenames on read" now asserts the new distinguishable reasons (:invalid_filename, :tree_hash_mismatch) instead of the old opaque :invalid_entry, preserving the same load-bearing "bad path fails" / "tree_hash mismatch fails" checks.
Verification stubs flipped: none flipped false->true; specled.evidence_store.sync_entry_tolerance was added directly to the existing execute:true command-kind stub (`mix test test/specled_ex/evidence/sync_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_sync_task_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_prune_task_test.exs`).
Verification tier: change-scoped -- mix test test/specled_ex/evidence/sync_test.exs test/specled_ex/evidence/entry_test.exs test/specled_ex/evidence/store_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_sync_task_test.exs test/mix/tasks/spec_prune_task_test.exs (18 tests, 0 failures); mix test --stale (997 tests/3 properties, 0 failures); mix spec.check --no-run-commands (status=pass errors=0 warnings=0); mix format --check-formatted and mix deps.unlock --check-unused both clean. Full command-execution spec.check proof deferred to the stage merge gate.
Final-tier runs: 1
Deferred must requirements: none

Summary: Sync.entries/2 used to halt reconciliation for every peer on the first invalid path, undecodable JSON, or future/unsupported schema_version entry on the shared spec-evidence orphan branch. Sync now quarantines any single entry it cannot validate -- carried through the tree union byte-identical under its original path, with a warning naming the path and reason in the result's new `warnings` field -- while every other entry still reconciles normally; same-path quarantine conflicts between independently-created peers resolve deterministically (max of raw bytes) so convergence still holds. Entry.decode_file/2 now returns distinguishable reasons (:invalid_filename, :tree_hash_mismatch, :malformed_json, {:unsupported_schema_version, v}, :missing_schema_version) instead of one opaque :invalid_entry. Added specled.evidence_store.sync_entry_tolerance (must) plus a scenario to evidence_store.spec.md. Note: printing warnings via `mix spec.sync`/`mix spec.prune` CLI output was left undone since lib/mix/tasks/spec.sync.ex and lib/mix/tasks/spec.prune.ex are outside this ticket's Allowed touches -- Sync.run/2 already returns the data (matching the existing Store.record/2 warning-tuple convention), and I opened standalone follow-up specled_-6j7 to wire the two task call sites.

title: review-F6: one anomalous evidence blob wedges sync for every peer
